title: Past-only latent degradation state with uncertainty features
summary: Recent F1 modeling work shows state-space tyre models outperform simpler AR baselines and naturally treat pit stops as state resets.
rationale: Recent F1 modeling work shows state-space tyre models outperform simpler AR baselines and naturally treat pit stops as state resets. That suggests the useful signal is not raw TyreLife alone, but a filtered latent pace-loss state and its uncertainty within each driver-race stint.
implementation_hint: Within each Driver x Race_Year sequence, compute past-only filtered latent variables from LapTime, LapTime_Delta, TyreLife, and Cumulative_Degradation: estimated degradation slope, innovation shock, and posterior variance since last pit. Feed those few state features into the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Should sharpen detection of hidden cliff edges and distinguish genuinely degrading tyres from noisy laps, especially late in long stints.
risk: A full state-space filter may be computationally fiddly, and if approximated poorly it can add noise rather than signal.
